# Accessibility Checklist: Command, workflow, manifest, and file-layout reference

**Purpose**: Validate that DOC-007 requirements and planning artifacts define accessible generated reference pages, source-path links, static Markdown structure, and dense inventories for keyboard and assistive-technology users.
**Created**: 2026-06-17
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are generated reference pages required to use meaningful heading hierarchy for page titles, section groups, and per-record headings? [Resolved, Spec FR-016, FR-024, SC-009; Plan Generated Markdown Accessibility Constraints]
- [x] CHK002 Are generated lists or compact summary tables required to include meaningful labels or headings so dense inventories remain understandable outside visual layout? [Resolved, Spec FR-025, SC-009; Plan Generated Markdown Accessibility Constraints]
- [x] CHK003 Are source citation links required to use screen-reader-friendly link text that includes the repo-relative source path instead of ambiguous repeated labels? [Resolved, Spec FR-026, SC-009; Plan Generated Markdown Accessibility Constraints]
- [x] CHK004 Are generated pages required to remain readable and navigable as committed Markdown/static HTML without depending on JavaScript-only expansion, filtering, or client-side rendering? [Resolved, Spec FR-027, SC-009; Plan Generated Markdown Accessibility Constraints]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK005 Is the "stable section-per-record Markdown" structure specific enough to distinguish page sections, record headings, ordered labels, `Sources`, and `Inferred notes` for assistive technology? [Clarity, Spec FR-016, Data Model ReferenceRecord]
- [ ] CHK006 Is the required public source-link shape clear enough to keep source-path link text visible and unique across generated records? [Clarity, Spec FR-003, Session 2026-06-17 - Generation Format And Reviewability]
- [x] CHK007 Is "public-readable" clarified with accessibility expectations for generated pages that may contain many records? [Resolved, Spec FR-016, Session 2026-06-17 - Accessibility And Static Readability]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK008 Do the spec, data model, contract, and quickstart consistently require visible `Sources` fields for source facts? [Consistency, Spec FR-003, Data Model SourceCitation, Contract Content Contract, Quickstart Generate Reference Pages]
- [ ] CHK009 Do the generated Markdown and no-browser-execution boundaries align with the plan's static docs-site scope? [Consistency, Spec FR-014, Plan Constraints, Quickstart Validate Docs Site]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK010 Is there a measurable success criterion for accessible readability of dense generated inventories, such as meaningful headings, non-ambiguous link text, and no JavaScript-only disclosure dependency? [Resolved, Spec SC-009]
- [ ] CHK011 Can reviewers objectively verify that each source fact record exposes source paths as usable links to existing checked-in files? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-002]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK012 Are requirements defined for generated records with multiple source citations so repeated source links remain distinguishable to assistive technology? [Resolved, Spec FR-026, SC-009]
- [x] CHK013 Are requirements defined for generated pages with many records so keyboard users can scan headings and links without relying on visual table density? [Resolved, Spec FR-024, SC-009; Plan Generated Markdown Accessibility Constraints]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK014 Are the docs-site/static Markdown assumptions documented strongly enough to exclude JavaScript-only generated-page interactions from DOC-007? [Assumption, Spec Assumptions, Plan Technical Context]

## Verification Rerun - 2026-06-17

- [ ] CHK015 Are meaningful page, section, and per-record headings now required for generated dense inventories? [Completeness, Spec FR-016, FR-024, SC-009]
- [ ] CHK016 Are generated lists, summaries, and compact tables now required to have labels, headings, or table headers without becoming the only source-fact review surface? [Completeness, Spec FR-025]
- [ ] CHK017 Are citation links now required to expose repo-relative path text and distinguishing context for repeated or multiple citations? [Clarity, Spec FR-026, SC-009]
- [ ] CHK018 Are JavaScript-only expansion, filtering, disclosure, and client-rendered data explicitly excluded for required generated reference content? [Coverage, Spec FR-027, Plan Generated Markdown Accessibility Constraints]
- [ ] CHK019 Is "public-readable" now tied to accessibility-scannable generated prose for users, maintainers, and agents? [Clarity, Spec FR-016]
- [ ] CHK020 Is accessible dense-inventory readability now objectively measurable through a success criterion? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-009]
- [ ] CHK021 Are the spec and plan consistent about keeping broader accessibility automation and browser/responsive validation in DOC-010? [Scope, Spec Accessibility And Static Readability, Plan Generated Markdown Accessibility Constraints]
- [ ] CHK022 Does the accessibility remediation avoid implementation-code, plugin-runtime, generated-payload, and CI changes? [Consistency, Spec FR-015, FR-022; Plan Generated Markdown Accessibility Constraints]
